authors_text: Maged Abdalla Helmy Mohamed Abdou,Trung Tuyen Truong,Eric Jul,Paulo Ferreira
title: Deep Learning and Computer Vision Techniques for Microcirculation Analysis: A Review
title_zh: 用于微循环分析的深度学习和计算机视觉技术：综述
abstract: The analysis of microcirculation images has the potential to reveal early signs of life-threatening diseases like sepsis. Quantifying the capillary density and the capillary distribution in microcirculation images can be used as a biological marker to assist critically ill patients. The quantification of these biological markers is labor-intensive, time-consuming, and subject to interobserver variability. Several computer vision techniques with varying performance can be used to automate the analysis of these microcirculation images in light of the stated challenges. In this paper, we present a survey of over 50 research papers and present the most relevant and promising computer vision algorithms to automate the analysis of microcirculation images. Furthermore, we present a survey of the methods currently used by other researchers to automate the analysis of microcirculation images. This survey is of high clinical relevance because it acts as a guidebook of techniques for other researchers to develop their microcirculation analysis systems and algorithms.
summary: The analysis of microcirculation images has the potential to reveal early signs of life-threatening diseases like sepsis. Quantifying the capillary density and the capillary distribution in microcirculation images can be used as a biological marker to assist critically ill patients. The quantification of these biological markers is labor-intensive, time-consuming, and subject to interobserver variability. Several computer vision techniques with varying performance can be used to automate the analysis of these microcirculation images in light of the stated challenges. In this paper, we present a survey of over 50 research papers and present the most relevant and promising computer vision algorithms to automate the analysis of microcirculation images. Furthermore, we present a survey of the methods currently used by other researchers to automate the analysis of microcirculation images. This survey is of high clinical relevance because it acts as a guidebook of techniques for other researchers to develop their microcirculation analysis systems and algorithms.
abstract_zh: 微循环图像分析有可能揭示败血症等危及生命的疾病的早期迹象。量化微循环图像中的毛细血管密度和毛细血管分布可以作为帮助危重患者的生物标志物。这些生物标记物的量化是劳动密集型、耗时的，并且容易受到观察者间差异的影响。鉴于所面临的挑战，可以使用几种具有不同性能的计算机视觉技术来自动分析这些微循环图像。在本文中，我们对 50 多篇研究论文进行了调查，并提出了最相关和最有前途的计算机视觉算法来自动分析微循环图像。此外，我们还对其他研究人员目前使用的自动分析微循环图像的方法进行了调查。这项调查具有很高的临床相关性，因为它可以作为其他研究人员开发微循环分析系统和算法的技术指南。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了深度学习和计算机视觉技术在微循环图像自动化分析中的应用，旨在为相关研究提供技术指南。
AI.motivation: 手动分析微循环图像存在耗时、主观性强等问题，亟需自动化方法以提高临床诊断效率和准确性。
AI.method: 系统回顾了50余篇文献，分类总结了当前主流的计算机视觉算法及深度学习技术在微循环分析中的实现方法。
AI.result: 梳理了毛细血管密度量化、分布特征提取等关键技术，指出现有方法的性能差异及未来研究方向。
AI.conclusion: 该综述为微循环分析系统开发提供了技术路线参考，推动自动化诊断工具在临床危重症监测中的应用。

authors_text: Sera Buyukgoz,Jasmin Grosinger,Mohamed Chetouani,Alessandro Saffiotti
title: Two ways to make your robot proactive: reasoning about human intentions, or reasoning about possible futures
title_zh: 让机器人主动行动的两种方法：推理人类意图，或推理可能的未来
comment: 20 pages, 1-column, 4 figures
comments: 20 pages, 1-column, 4 figures
abstract: Robots sharing their space with humans need to be proactive in order to be helpful. Proactive robots are able to act on their own initiative in an anticipatory way to benefit humans. In this work, we investigate two ways to make robots proactive. One way is to recognize humans' intentions and to act to fulfill them, like opening the door that you are about to cross. The other way is to reason about possible future threats or opportunities and to act to prevent or to foster them, like recommending you to take an umbrella since rain has been forecasted. In this paper, we present approaches to realize these two types of proactive behavior. We then present an integrated system that can generate proactive robot behavior by reasoning on both factors: intentions and predictions. We illustrate our system on a sample use case including a domestic robot and a human. We first run this use case with the two separate proactive systems, intention-based and prediction-based, and then run it with our integrated system. The results show that the integrated system is able to take into account a broader variety of aspects that are needed for proactivity.
summary: Robots sharing their space with humans need to be proactive in order to be helpful. Proactive robots are able to act on their own initiative in an anticipatory way to benefit humans. In this work, we investigate two ways to make robots proactive. One way is to recognize humans' intentions and to act to fulfill them, like opening the door that you are about to cross. The other way is to reason about possible future threats or opportunities and to act to prevent or to foster them, like recommending you to take an umbrella since rain has been forecasted. In this paper, we present approaches to realize these two types of proactive behavior. We then present an integrated system that can generate proactive robot behavior by reasoning on both factors: intentions and predictions. We illustrate our system on a sample use case including a domestic robot and a human. We first run this use case with the two separate proactive systems, intention-based and prediction-based, and then run it with our integrated system. The results show that the integrated system is able to take into account a broader variety of aspects that are needed for proactivity.
abstract_zh: 与人类共享空间的机器人需要积极主动才能提供帮助。主动型机器人能够以预期的方式主动采取行动，造福人类。在这项工作中，我们研究了两种让机器人主动行动的方法。一种方法是认识人类的意图并采取行动来实现它们，就像打开你即将穿过的门。另一种方法是推理未来可能的威胁或机会，并采取行动预防或促进它们，例如由于预报有雨而建议您带伞。在本文中，我们提出了实现这两类主动行为的方法。然后，我们提出了一个集成系统，可以通过推理意图和预测这两个因素来生成主动的机器人行为。我们通过一个示例用例（包括家用机器人和人类）来说明我们的系统。我们首先使用两个独立的主动系统（基于意图和基于预测）运行此用例，然后使用我们的集成系统运行它。结果表明，集成系统能够考虑主动性所需的更广泛的方面。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了两种使机器人具备主动性的方法：通过推理人类意图或可能的未来，并提出一个集成系统以实现更全面的主动性行为。
AI.motivation: 机器人需要主动行为以在与人类共享空间时提供帮助，但现有方法可能无法全面考虑人类意图和未来预测，因此需要更综合的解决方案。
AI.method: 提出两种方法：识别人类意图并采取行动（如开门），以及预测未来并采取措施（如建议带伞），并开发一个集成系统，结合意图推理和未来预测生成主动行为。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，集成系统能综合考虑更多因素，生成更有效的主动行为，优于单独使用意图或预测的方法。
AI.conclusion: 集成系统通过结合人类意图推理和未来预测，能够更全面地实现机器人主动性，提升其在复杂环境中的适应能力。

method: 提出两种方法：识别人类意图并采取行动（如开门），以及预测未来并采取措施（如建议带伞），并开发一个集成系统，结合意图推理和未来预测生成主动行为。
result: 实验结果表明，集成系统能综合考虑更多因素，生成更有效的主动行为，优于单独使用意图或预测的方法。
conclusion: 集成系统通过结合人类意图推理和未来预测，能够更全面地实现机器人主动性，提升其在复杂环境中的适应能力。
search_text: Two ways to make your robot proactive: reasoning about human intentions, or reasoning about possible futures 让机器人主动行动的两种方法：推理人类意图，或推理可能的未来 Robots sharing their space with humans need to be proactive in order to be helpful. Proactive robots are able to act on their own initiative in an anticipatory way to benefit humans. In this work, we investigate two ways to make robots proactive. One way is to recognize humans' intentions and to act to fulfill them, like opening the door that you are about to cross. The other way is to reason about possible future threats or opportunities and to act to prevent or to foster them, like recommending you to take an umbrella since rain has been forecasted. In this paper, we present approaches to realize these two types of proactive behavior. We then present an integrated system that can generate proactive robot behavior by reasoning on both factors: intentions and predictions. We illustrate our system on a sample use case including a domestic robot and a human. We first run this use case with the two separate proactive systems, intention-based and prediction-based, and then run it with our integrated system. The results show that the integrated system is able to take into account a broader variety of aspects that are needed for proactivity. 与人类共享空间的机器人需要积极主动才能提供帮助。主动型机器人能够以预期的方式主动采取行动，造福人类。在这项工作中，我们研究了两种让机器人主动行动的方法。一种方法是认识人类的意图并采取行动来实现它们，就像打开你即将穿过的门。另一种方法是推理未来可能的威胁或机会，并采取行动预防或促进它们，例如由于预报有雨而建议您带伞。在本文中，我们提出了实现这两类主动行为的方法。然后，我们提出了一个集成系统，可以通过推理意图和预测这两个因素来生成主动的机器人行为。我们通过一个示例用例（包括家用机器人和人类）来说明我们的系统。我们首先使用两个独立的主动系统（基于意图和基于预测）运行此用例，然后使用我们的集成系统运行它。结果表明，集成系统能够考虑主动性所需的更广泛的方面。 本文探讨了两种使机器人具备主动性的方法：通过推理人类意图或可能的未来，并提出一个集成系统以实现更全面的主动性行为。 Sera Buyukgoz,Jasmin Grosinger,Mohamed Chetouani,Alessandro Saffiotti cs.AI,cs.RO

authors_text: Jasmine Quah,Omer Sella,Thomas Heinis
title: DNA data storage, sequencing data-carrying DNA
title_zh: DNA数据存储、携带DNA测序数据
abstract: DNA is a leading candidate as the next archival storage media due to its density, durability and sustainability. To read (and write) data DNA storage exploits technology that has been developed over decades to sequence naturally occurring DNA in the life sciences. To achieve higher accuracy for previously unseen, biological DNA, sequencing relies on extending and training deep machine learning models known as basecallers. This growth in model complexity requires substantial resources, both computational and data sets. It also eliminates the possibility of a compact read head for DNA as a storage medium. We argue that we need to depart from blindly using sequencing models from the life sciences for DNA data storage. The difference is striking: for life science applications we have no control over the DNA, however, in the case of DNA data storage, we control how it is written, as well as the particular write head. More specifically, data-carrying DNA can be modulated and embedded with alignment markers and error correcting codes to guarantee higher fidelity and to carry out some of the work that the machine learning models perform. In this paper, we study accuracy trade-offs between deep model size and error correcting codes. We show that, starting with a model size of 107MB, the reduced accuracy from model compression can be compensated by using simple error correcting codes in the DNA sequences. In our experiments, we show that a substantial reduction in the size of the model does not incur an undue penalty for the error correcting codes used, therefore paving the way for portable data-carrying DNA read head. Crucially, we show that through the joint use of model compression and error correcting codes, we achieve a higher read accuracy than without compression and error correction codes.
summary: DNA is a leading candidate as the next archival storage media due to its density, durability and sustainability. To read (and write) data DNA storage exploits technology that has been developed over decades to sequence naturally occurring DNA in the life sciences. To achieve higher accuracy for previously unseen, biological DNA, sequencing relies on extending and training deep machine learning models known as basecallers. This growth in model complexity requires substantial resources, both computational and data sets. It also eliminates the possibility of a compact read head for DNA as a storage medium. We argue that we need to depart from blindly using sequencing models from the life sciences for DNA data storage. The difference is striking: for life science applications we have no control over the DNA, however, in the case of DNA data storage, we control how it is written, as well as the particular write head. More specifically, data-carrying DNA can be modulated and embedded with alignment markers and error correcting codes to guarantee higher fidelity and to carry out some of the work that the machine learning models perform. In this paper, we study accuracy trade-offs between deep model size and error correcting codes. We show that, starting with a model size of 107MB, the reduced accuracy from model compression can be compensated by using simple error correcting codes in the DNA sequences. In our experiments, we show that a substantial reduction in the size of the model does not incur an undue penalty for the error correcting codes used, therefore paving the way for portable data-carrying DNA read head. Crucially, we show that through the joint use of model compression and error correcting codes, we achieve a higher read accuracy than without compression and error correction codes.
abstract_zh: DNA 因其密度、耐用性和可持续性而成为下一代档案存储介质的领先候选者。为了读取（和写入）数据，DNA 存储利用了数十年来开发的技术，对生命科学中自然发生的 DNA 进行测序。为了对以前未见过的生物 DNA 实现更高的准确性，测序依赖于扩展和训练称为碱基识别器的深度机器学习模型。模型复杂性的增长需要大量资源，包括计算资源和数据集。它还消除了使用紧凑型 DNA 读取头作为存储介质的可能性。我们认为，我们需要摆脱盲目使用生命科学测序模型进行 DNA 数据存储的做法。差异是惊人的：对于生命科学应用，我们无法控制 DNA，但是，在 DNA 数据存储的情况下，我们可以控制它的写入方式以及特定的写入头。更具体地说，可以对携带数据的 DNA 进行调制并嵌入对齐标记和纠错码，以保证更高的保真度并执行机器学习模型执行的一些工作。在本文中，我们研究深度模型大小和纠错码之间的准确性权衡。我们证明，从 107MB 的模型大小开始，可以通过在 DNA 序列中使用简单的纠错码来补偿模型压缩带来的精度降低。在我们的实验中，我们表明模型尺寸的大幅减小不会对所使用的纠错码造成过度的损失，因此为便携式数据携带 DNA 读取头铺平了道路。至关重要的是，我们表明，通过联合使用模型压缩和纠错码，我们比不使用压缩和纠错码实现了更高的读取精度。

AI.tldr: 本文研究如何通过模型压缩与纠错码结合提升DNA数据存储的读取准确性，以实现便携式DNA读取头。
AI.motivation: 传统生命科学领域的DNA测序模型无法满足DNA数据存储需求，因其缺乏对DNA写入过程的控制，导致模型复杂且难以小型化。
AI.method: 通过压缩深度学习模型（基线模型）并结合DNA序列中的纠错码，分析模型规模缩减与纠错码对读取精度的影响。
AI.result: 实验表明，107MB的压缩模型结合简单纠错码可实现与原模型相当的读取精度，显著降低模型资源需求。
AI.conclusion: 模型压缩与纠错码的联合使用可提升DNA数据存储的读取准确性，为便携式DNA读取设备提供可行方案。

authors_text: Haoqian Wu,Keyu Chen,Yanan Luo,Ruizhi Qiao,Bo Ren,Haozhe Liu,Weicheng Xie,Linlin Shen
title: Scene Consistency Representation Learning for Video Scene Segmentation
title_zh: 视频场景分割的场景一致性表示学习
comment: Accepted to CVPR 2022
comments: Accepted to CVPR 2022
abstract: A long-term video, such as a movie or TV show, is composed of various scenes, each of which represents a series of shots sharing the same semantic story. Spotting the correct scene boundary from the long-term video is a challenging task, since a model must understand the storyline of the video to figure out where a scene starts and ends. To this end, we propose an effective Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) framework to learn better shot representations from unlabeled long-term videos. More specifically, we present an SSL scheme to achieve scene consistency, while exploring considerable data augmentation and shuffling methods to boost the model generalizability. Instead of explicitly learning the scene boundary features as in the previous methods, we introduce a vanilla temporal model with less inductive bias to verify the quality of the shot features. Our method achieves the state-of-the-art performance on the task of Video Scene Segmentation. Additionally, we suggest a more fair and reasonable benchmark to evaluate the performance of Video Scene Segmentation methods. The code is made available.
summary: A long-term video, such as a movie or TV show, is composed of various scenes, each of which represents a series of shots sharing the same semantic story. Spotting the correct scene boundary from the long-term video is a challenging task, since a model must understand the storyline of the video to figure out where a scene starts and ends. To this end, we propose an effective Self-Supervised Learning (SSL) framework to learn better shot representations from unlabeled long-term videos. More specifically, we present an SSL scheme to achieve scene consistency, while exploring considerable data augmentation and shuffling methods to boost the model generalizability. Instead of explicitly learning the scene boundary features as in the previous methods, we introduce a vanilla temporal model with less inductive bias to verify the quality of the shot features. Our method achieves the state-of-the-art performance on the task of Video Scene Segmentation. Additionally, we suggest a more fair and reasonable benchmark to evaluate the performance of Video Scene Segmentation methods. The code is made available.
abstract_zh: 长视频（例如电影或电视节目）由各种场景组成，每个场景代表一系列共享相同语义故事的镜头。从长期视频中找出正确的场景边界是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为模型必须理解视频的故事情节才能找出场景的开始和结束位置。为此，我们提出了一种有效的自监督学习（SSL）框架，以从未标记的长期视频中学习更好的镜头表示。更具体地说，我们提出了一种 SSL 方案来实现场景一致性，同时探索大量的数据增强和洗牌方法来提高模型的通用性。我们没有像以前的方法那样显式地学习场景边界特征，而是引入了一种具有较少归纳偏差的普通时间模型来验证镜头特征的质量。我们的方法在视频场景分割任务上实现了最先进的性能。此外，我们建议一个更公平合理的基准来评估视频场景分割方法的性能。该代码已可用。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种用于视频场景分割的场景一致性表示学习方法。
AI.motivation: 长期视频（如电影或电视剧）由多个场景组成，每个场景代表一个共享相同语义故事的一系列镜头。正确识别视频中的场景边界是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为模型必须理解视频的情节才能确定场景的起始和结束。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种自监督学习（SSL）框架，用于从未标记的长期视频中学习更好的镜头表示。通过探索大量的数据增强和洗牌方法来增强模型的泛化能力，并引入了一个具有较少归纳偏置的普通时间模型来验证镜头特征的质量。
AI.result: 该方法在视频场景分割任务上达到了最先进的性能，并建议了一个更公平和合理的基准来评估视频场景分割方法的性能。
AI.conclusion: 本文提出的方法有效提升了视频场景分割的性能，并为该任务提供了更合理的评估基准。

Our method achieves the state-of-the-art performance on the task of Video Scene Segmentation. Additionally, we suggest a more fair and reasonable benchmark to evaluate the performance of Video Scene Segmentation methods. The code is made available. 长视频（例如电影或电视节目）由各种场景组成，每个场景代表一系列共享相同语义故事的镜头。从长期视频中找出正确的场景边界是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为模型必须理解视频的故事情节才能找出场景的开始和结束位置。为此，我们提出了一种有效的自监督学习（SSL）框架，以从未标记的长期视频中学习更好的镜头表示。更具体地说，我们提出了一种 SSL 方案来实现场景一致性，同时探索大量的数据增强和洗牌方法来提高模型的通用性。我们没有像以前的方法那样显式地学习场景边界特征，而是引入了一种具有较少归纳偏差的普通时间模型来验证镜头特征的质量。我们的方法在视频场景分割任务上实现了最先进的性能。此外，我们建议一个更公平合理的基准来评估视频场景分割方法的性能。该代码已可用。 本文提出了一种用于视频场景分割的场景一致性表示学习方法。 Haoqian Wu,Keyu Chen,Yanan Luo,Ruizhi Qiao,Bo Ren,Haozhe Liu,Weicheng Xie,Linlin Shen cs.CV,cs.AI

authors_text: Madhurananda Pahar,Marisa Klopper,Byron Reeve,Rob Warren,Grant Theron,Andreas Diacon,Thomas Niesler
title: Automatic Tuberculosis and COVID-19 cough classification using deep learning
title_zh: 使用深度学习自动对结核病和 COVID-19 咳嗽进行分类
comment: This paper has been published in 2022 International Conference on Electrical, Computer and Energy Technologies (ICECET)
comments: This paper has been published in 2022 International Conference on Electrical, Computer and Energy Technologies (ICECET)
abstract: We present a deep learning based automatic cough classifier which can discriminate tuberculosis (TB) coughs from COVID-19 coughs and healthy coughs. Both TB and COVID-19 are respiratory diseases, contagious, have cough as a predominant symptom and claim thousands of lives each year. The cough audio recordings were collected at both indoor and outdoor settings and also uploaded using smartphones from subjects around the globe, thus containing various levels of noise. This cough data include 1.68 hours of TB coughs, 18.54 minutes of COVID-19 coughs and 1.69 hours of healthy coughs from 47 TB patients, 229 COVID-19 patients and 1498 healthy patients and were used to train and evaluate a CNN, LSTM and Resnet50. These three deep architectures were also pre-trained on 2.14 hours of sneeze, 2.91 hours of speech and 2.79 hours of noise for improved performance. The class-imbalance in our dataset was addressed by using SMOTE data balancing technique and using performance metrics such as F1-score and AUC. Our study shows that the highest F1-scores of 0.9259 and 0.8631 have been achieved from a pre-trained Resnet50 for two-class (TB vs COVID-19) and three-class (TB vs COVID-19 vs healthy) cough classification tasks, respectively. The application of deep transfer learning has improved the classifiers' performance and makes them more robust as they generalise better over the cross-validation folds. Their performances exceed the TB triage test requirements set by the world health organisation (WHO). The features producing the best performance contain higher order of MFCCs suggesting that the differences between TB and COVID-19 coughs are not perceivable by the human ear. This type of cough audio classification is non-contact, cost-effective and can easily be deployed on a smartphone, thus it can be an excellent tool for both TB and COVID-19 screening.

This cough data include 1.68 hours of TB coughs, 18.54 minutes of COVID-19 coughs and 1.69 hours of healthy coughs from 47 TB patients, 229 COVID-19 patients and 1498 healthy patients and were used to train and evaluate a CNN, LSTM and Resnet50. These three deep architectures were also pre-trained on 2.14 hours of sneeze, 2.91 hours of speech and 2.79 hours of noise for improved performance. The class-imbalance in our dataset was addressed by using SMOTE data balancing technique and using performance metrics such as F1-score and AUC. Our study shows that the highest F1-scores of 0.9259 and 0.8631 have been achieved from a pre-trained Resnet50 for two-class (TB vs COVID-19) and three-class (TB vs COVID-19 vs healthy) cough classification tasks, respectively. The application of deep transfer learning has improved the classifiers' performance and makes them more robust as they generalise better over the cross-validation folds. Their performances exceed the TB triage test requirements set by the world health organisation (WHO). The features producing the best performance contain higher order of MFCCs suggesting that the differences between TB and COVID-19 coughs are not perceivable by the human ear. abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种基于深度学习的自动咳嗽分类器，可以区分结核病 (TB) 咳嗽、COVID-19 咳嗽和健康咳嗽。结核病和 COVID-19 都是呼吸道疾病，具有传染性，以咳嗽为主要症状，每年夺去数千人的生命。咳嗽录音是在室内和室外环境下收集的，并使用智能手机从全球各地的受试者上传，因此包含不同程度的噪音。该咳嗽数据包括来自 47 名结核病患者、229 名 COVID-19 患者和 1498 名健康患者的 1.68 小时结核病咳嗽、18.54 分钟 COVID-19 咳嗽和 1.69 小时健康咳嗽，并用于训练和评估 CNN、LSTM 和 Resnet50。这三种深度架构还针对 2.14 小时的喷嚏、2.91 小时的讲话和 2.79 小时的噪音进行了预训练，以提高性能。我们的数据集中的类不平衡问题是通过使用 SMOTE 数据平衡技术并使用 F1 分数和 AUC 等性能指标来解决的。我们的研究表明，针对两类（TB vs COVID-19）和三类（TB vs COVID-19 vs 健康）咳嗽分类任务的预训练 Resnet50 分别获得了最高的 F1 分数 0.9259 和 0.8631。深度迁移学习的应用提高了分类器的性能，并使它们更加稳健，因为它们在交叉验证折叠上具有更好的泛化能力。它们的表现超出了世界卫生组织 (WHO) 规定的结核病分类测试要求。产生最佳性能的特征包含更高阶的 MFCC，表明人耳无法感知 TB 和 COVID-19 咳嗽之间的差异。这种类型的咳嗽音频分类是非接触式的、经济高效的，并且可以轻松部署在智能手机上，因此它可以成为结核病和 COVID-19 筛查的绝佳工具。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于深度学习的自动咳嗽分类器，用于区分肺结核（TB）、COVID-19咳嗽及健康咳嗽。
AI.motivation: TB和COVID-19均为高致死性呼吸道传染病，咳嗽为典型症状。传统筛查方法存在接触性、成本高等问题，需开发非接触、低成本的智能分类工具。
AI.method: 采用CNN、LSTM和ResNet50三种深度模型，结合预训练的喷嚏、语音和噪声数据。使用SMOTE技术解决类别不平衡问题，并通过F1-score和AUC评估性能。
AI.result: 预训练ResNet50在二分类（TB vs COVID-19）和三分类（TB/COVID-19/健康）任务中分别达到0.9259和0.8631的F1-score，性能超越WHO设定的TB分诊标准。
AI.conclusion: 深度迁移学习显著提升分类器鲁棒性，高阶MFCC特征揭示TB与COVID-19咳嗽的细微差异，该方法可部署于智能手机，成为有效的传染病筛查工具。

authors_text: Paolo De Petris,Shehryar Khattak,Mihir Dharmadhikari,Gabriel Waibel,Huan Nguyen,Markus Montenegro,Nikhil Khedekar,Kostas Alexis,Marco Hutter
title: Marsupial Walking-and-Flying Robotic Deployment for Collaborative Exploration of Unknown Environments
title_zh: 用于协作探索未知环境的有袋动物步行和飞行机器人部署
comment: 6 pages, 6 figures, Submitted to the IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems, 2022
comments: 6 pages, 6 figures, Submitted to the IEEE/RSJ International Conference on Intelligent Robots and Systems, 2022
abstract: This work contributes a marsupial robotic system-of-systems involving a legged and an aerial robot capable of collaborative mapping and exploration path planning that exploits the heterogeneous properties of the two systems and the ability to selectively deploy the aerial system from the ground robot. Exploiting the dexterous locomotion capabilities and long endurance of quadruped robots, the marsupial combination can explore within large-scale and confined environments involving rough terrain. However, as certain types of terrain or vertical geometries can render any ground system unable to continue its exploration, the marsupial system can - when needed - deploy the flying robot which, by exploiting its 3D navigation capabilities, can undertake a focused exploration task within its endurance limitations. Focusing on autonomy, the two systems can co-localize and map together by sharing LiDAR-based maps and plan exploration paths individually, while a tailored graph search onboard the legged robot allows it to identify where and when the ferried aerial platform should be deployed. The system is verified within multiple experimental studies demonstrating the expanded exploration capabilities of the marsupial system-of-systems and facilitating the exploration of otherwise individually unreachable areas.
summary: This work contributes a marsupial robotic system-of-systems involving a legged and an aerial robot capable of collaborative mapping and exploration path planning that exploits the heterogeneous properties of the two systems and the ability to selectively deploy the aerial system from the ground robot. Exploiting the dexterous locomotion capabilities and long endurance of quadruped robots, the marsupial combination can explore within large-scale and confined environments involving rough terrain. However, as certain types of terrain or vertical geometries can render any ground system unable to continue its exploration, the marsupial system can - when needed - deploy the flying robot which, by exploiting its 3D navigation capabilities, can undertake a focused exploration task within its endurance limitations. Focusing on autonomy, the two systems can co-localize and map together by sharing LiDAR-based maps and plan exploration paths individually, while a tailored graph search onboard the legged robot allows it to identify where and when the ferried aerial platform should be deployed. The system is verified within multiple experimental studies demonstrating the expanded exploration capabilities of the marsupial system-of-systems and facilitating the exploration of otherwise individually unreachable areas.
abstract_zh: 这项工作贡献了一个有袋类机器人​​系统，包括一个有腿机器人和一个空中机器人，能够进行协作测绘和探索路径规划，利用两个系统的异构特性以及从地面机器人选择性部署空中系统的能力。利用四足机器人灵巧的运动能力和长时间的耐力，有袋动物组合可以在涉及崎岖地形的大范围和有限环境中进行探索。然而，由于某些类型的地形或垂直几何形状可能使任何地面系统无法继续探索，有袋动物系统可以在需要时部署飞行机器人，该机器人通过利用其 3D 导航功能，可以在其耐力限制内承担集中的探索任务。专注于自主性，这两个系统可以通过共享基于激光雷达的地图来共同定位和绘制地图，并单独规划探索路径，而腿式机器人上的定制图形搜索使其能够识别应该在何时何地部署渡轮空中平台。该系统在多项实验研究中得到验证，证明了有袋动物系统的扩展探索能力，并促进了对其他单独无法到达的区域的探索。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种由地面四足机器人和空中无人机组成的‘袋鼠’机器人系统，用于协作探索未知环境。
AI.motivation: 地面机器人在复杂地形和狭窄空间中具有良好的移动性和持久性，但在某些垂直或特殊地形中无法继续探索，因此需要结合空中无人机进行补充。
AI.method: 通过结合四足机器人和无人机的异构特性，利用LiDAR技术进行协同定位和地图构建，并通过图搜索算法决定无人机的部署时机和位置。
AI.result: 实验表明，该系统能够扩展探索能力，完成单独机器人无法达到的区域。
AI.conclusion: 该‘袋鼠’系统通过协作探索，有效提升了在复杂和未知环境中的探索能力。

search_text: Marsupial Walking-and-Flying Robotic Deployment for Collaborative Exploration of Unknown Environments 用于协作探索未知环境的有袋动物步行和飞行机器人部署 This work contributes a marsupial robotic system-of-systems involving a legged and an aerial robot capable of collaborative mapping and exploration path planning that exploits the heterogeneous properties of the two systems and the ability to selectively deploy the aerial system from the ground robot. Exploiting the dexterous locomotion capabilities and long endurance of quadruped robots, the marsupial combination can explore within large-scale and confined environments involving rough terrain. However, as certain types of terrain or vertical geometries can render any ground system unable to continue its exploration, the marsupial system can - when needed - deploy the flying robot which, by exploiting its 3D navigation capabilities, can undertake a focused exploration task within its endurance limitations. Focusing on autonomy, the two systems can co-localize and map together by sharing LiDAR-based maps and plan exploration paths individually, while a tailored graph search onboard the legged robot allows it to identify where and when the ferried aerial platform should be deployed. The system is verified within multiple experimental studies demonstrating the expanded exploration capabilities of the marsupial system-of-systems and facilitating the exploration of otherwise individually unreachable areas. 这项工作贡献了一个有袋类机器人​​系统，包括一个有腿机器人和一个空中机器人，能够进行协作测绘和探索路径规划，利用两个系统的异构特性以及从地面机器人选择性部署空中系统的能力。利用四足机器人灵巧的运动能力和长时间的耐力，有袋动物组合可以在涉及崎岖地形的大范围和有限环境中进行探索。然而，由于某些类型的地形或垂直几何形状可能使任何地面系统无法继续探索，有袋动物系统可以在需要时部署飞行机器人，该机器人通过利用其 3D 导航功能，可以在其耐力限制内承担集中的探索任务。专注于自主性，这两个系统可以通过共享基于激光雷达的地图来共同定位和绘制地图，并单独规划探索路径，而腿式机器人上的定制图形搜索使其能够识别应该在何时何地部署渡轮空中平台。该系统在多项实验研究中得到验证，证明了有袋动物系统的扩展探索能力，并促进了对其他单独无法到达的区域的探索。 本文提出了一种由地面四足机器人和空中无人机组成的‘袋鼠’机器人系统，用于协作探索未知环境。 Paolo De Petris,Shehryar Khattak,Mihir Dharmadhikari,Gabriel Waibel,Huan Nguyen,Markus Montenegro,Nikhil Khedekar,Kostas Alexis,Marco Hutter cs.RO

authors_text: Tommaso Barletti,Niccolo' Biondi,Federico Pernici,Matteo Bruni,Alberto Del Bimbo
title: Contrastive Supervised Distillation for Continual Representation Learning
title_zh: 持续表征学习的对比监督蒸馏
comment: Paper published as Oral and awarded as Best Student Paper at ICIAP21
comments: Paper published as Oral and awarded as Best Student Paper at ICIAP21
abstract: In this paper, we propose a novel training procedure for the continual representation learning problem in which a neural network model is sequentially learned to alleviate catastrophic forgetting in visual search tasks. Our method, called Contrastive Supervised Distillation (CSD), reduces feature forgetting while learning discriminative features. This is achieved by leveraging labels information in a distillation setting in which the student model is contrastively learned from the teacher model. Extensive experiments show that CSD performs favorably in mitigating catastrophic forgetting by outperforming current state-of-the-art methods. Our results also provide further evidence that feature forgetting evaluated in visual retrieval tasks is not as catastrophic as in classification tasks. Code at: https://github.com/NiccoBiondi/ContrastiveSupervisedDistillation.
summary: In this paper, we propose a novel training procedure for the continual representation learning problem in which a neural network model is sequentially learned to alleviate catastrophic forgetting in visual search tasks. Our method, called Contrastive Supervised Distillation (CSD), reduces feature forgetting while learning discriminative features. This is achieved by leveraging labels information in a distillation setting in which the student model is contrastively learned from the teacher model. Extensive experiments show that CSD performs favorably in mitigating catastrophic forgetting by outperforming current state-of-the-art methods. Our results also provide further evidence that feature forgetting evaluated in visual retrieval tasks is not as catastrophic as in classification tasks. Code at: https://github.com/NiccoBiondi/ContrastiveSupervisedDistillation.
abstract_zh: 在本文中，我们针对连续表示学习问题提出了一种新颖的训练过程，其中顺序学习神经网络模型以减轻视觉搜索任务中的灾难性遗忘。我们的方法称为对比监督蒸馏（CSD），可以在学习判别性特征时减少特征遗忘。这是通过在蒸馏设置中利用标签信息来实现的，其中学生模型是从教师模型中对比学习的。大量实验表明，CSD 优于当前最先进的方法，在减轻灾难性遗忘方面表现良好。我们的结果还提供了进一步的证据，表明在视觉检索任务中评估的特征遗忘并不像分类任务中那样灾难性。代码位于：https://github.com/NiccoBiondi/ContrastiveSupervisedDistillation。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为对比监督蒸馏（CSD）的新方法，用于解决视觉搜索任务中的持续表征学习中的灾难性遗忘问题。
AI.motivation: 在持续学习中，模型在学习新任务时容易遗忘旧知识（灾难性遗忘），尤其在视觉搜索任务中，现有方法难以有效缓解这一问题，因此需要一种新的方法来利用标签信息减少特征遗忘。
AI.method: 提出对比监督蒸馏（CSD）方法，通过在蒸馏过程中利用标签信息，让学生模型在对比学习框架下从教师模型中学习，从而减少特征遗忘并提取判别性特征。
AI.result: 实验表明，CSD在缓解灾难性遗忘方面优于现有最先进方法，并证实了视觉检索任务中的特征遗忘程度低于分类任务。
AI.conclusion: CSD有效缓解了持续学习中的灾难性遗忘，且视觉检索任务中的特征遗忘问题比分类任务中更不严重。

authors_text: Hendrik Schröter,Alberto N. Escalante-B.,Tobias Rosenkranz,Andreas Maier
title: DeepFilterNet2: Towards Real-Time Speech Enhancement on Embedded Devices for Full-Band Audio
title_zh: DeepFilterNet2：在嵌入式设备上实现全频带音频的实时语音增强
comment: Submitted to IWAENC 2022
comments: Submitted to IWAENC 2022
abstract: Deep learning-based speech enhancement has seen huge improvements and recently also expanded to full band audio (48 kHz). However, many approaches have a rather high computational complexity and require big temporal buffers for real time usage e.g. due to temporal convolutions or attention. Both make those approaches not feasible on embedded devices. This work further extends DeepFilterNet, which exploits harmonic structure of speech allowing for efficient speech enhancement (SE). Several optimizations in the training procedure, data augmentation, and network structure result in state-of-the-art SE performance while reducing the real-time factor to 0.04 on a notebook Core-i5 CPU. This makes the algorithm applicable to run on embedded devices in real-time. The DeepFilterNet framework can be obtained under an open source license.
summary: Deep learning-based speech enhancement has seen huge improvements and recently also expanded to full band audio (48 kHz). However, many approaches have a rather high computational complexity and require big temporal buffers for real time usage e.g. due to temporal convolutions or attention. Both make those approaches not feasible on embedded devices. This work further extends DeepFilterNet, which exploits harmonic structure of speech allowing for efficient speech enhancement (SE). Several optimizations in the training procedure, data augmentation, and network structure result in state-of-the-art SE performance while reducing the real-time factor to 0.04 on a notebook Core-i5 CPU. This makes the algorithm applicable to run on embedded devices in real-time. The DeepFilterNet framework can be obtained under an open source license.
abstract_zh: 基于深度学习的语音增强技术已经取得了巨大的进步，最近还扩展到全频带音频 (48 kHz)。然而，许多方法具有相当高的计算复杂性，并且需要大的时间缓冲区来实时使用，例如由于时间卷积或注意力。两者都使得这些方法在嵌入式设备上不可行。这项工作进一步扩展了 DeepFilterNet，它利用语音的谐波结构来实现高效的语音增强 (SE)。训练过程、数据增强和网络结构方面的多项优化带来了最先进的 SE 性能，同时在笔记本 Core-i5 CPU 上将实时因子降低至 0.04。这使得该算法适用于在嵌入式设备上实时运行。 DeepFilterNet 框架可以通过开源许可证获得。

AI.tldr: 本论文提出DeepFilterNet2框架，旨在实现适用于嵌入式设备的全带音频实时语音增强。
AI.motivation: 现有深度学习语音增强方法计算复杂度高且需大时间缓冲区，难以在嵌入式设备上实时运行。
AI.method: 通过优化训练流程、数据增强及网络结构，利用语音谐波特性提升DeepFilterNet效率。
AI.result: 在笔记本Core-i5 CPU上实现0.04实时因子，达到SOTA性能，适用于嵌入式设备。
AI.conclusion: DeepFilterNet2在保持高性能的同时降低计算需求，且框架已开源，适合嵌入式部署。

authors_text: Chuqiao Li,Zhiwu Huang,Danda Pani Paudel,Yabin Wang,Mohamad Shahbazi,Xiaopeng Hong,Luc Van Gool
title: A Continual Deepfake Detection Benchmark: Dataset, Methods, and Essentials
title_zh: 持续的 Deepfake 检测基准：数据集、方法和要点
comment: Accepted to WACV 2023
comments: Accepted to WACV 2023
abstract: There have been emerging a number of benchmarks and techniques for the detection of deepfakes. However, very few works study the detection of incrementally appearing deepfakes in the real-world scenarios. To simulate the wild scenes, this paper suggests a continual deepfake detection benchmark (CDDB) over a new collection of deepfakes from both known and unknown generative models. The suggested CDDB designs multiple evaluations on the detection over easy, hard, and long sequence of deepfake tasks, with a set of appropriate measures. In addition, we exploit multiple approaches to adapt multiclass incremental learning methods, commonly used in the continual visual recognition, to the continual deepfake detection problem. We evaluate existing methods, including their adapted ones, on the proposed CDDB. Within the proposed benchmark, we explore some commonly known essentials of standard continual learning. Our study provides new insights on these essentials in the context of continual deepfake detection. The suggested CDDB is clearly more challenging than the existing benchmarks, which thus offers a suitable evaluation avenue to the future research. Both data and code are available at https://github.com/Coral79/CDDB.
summary: There have been emerging a number of benchmarks and techniques for the detection of deepfakes. However, very few works study the detection of incrementally appearing deepfakes in the real-world scenarios. To simulate the wild scenes, this paper suggests a continual deepfake detection benchmark (CDDB) over a new collection of deepfakes from both known and unknown generative models. The suggested CDDB designs multiple evaluations on the detection over easy, hard, and long sequence of deepfake tasks, with a set of appropriate measures. In addition, we exploit multiple approaches to adapt multiclass incremental learning methods, commonly used in the continual visual recognition, to the continual deepfake detection problem. We evaluate existing methods, including their adapted ones, on the proposed CDDB. Within the proposed benchmark, we explore some commonly known essentials of standard continual learning. Our study provides new insights on these essentials in the context of continual deepfake detection. The suggested CDDB is clearly more challenging than the existing benchmarks, which thus offers a suitable evaluation avenue to the future research. Both data and code are available at https://github.com/Coral79/CDDB.
abstract_zh: 已经出现了许多用于检测深度赝品的基准和技术。然而，很少有工作研究在现实场景中检测逐渐出现的深度赝品。为了模拟野外场景，本文提出了一个针对已知和未知生成模型的新深度换脸集合的连续深度换脸检测基准（CDDB）。建议的 CDDB 设计了对简单、困难和长序列的 Deepfake 任务的检测的多重评估，并采取了一组适当的措施。此外，我们利用多种方法来适应连续视觉识别中常用的多类增量学习方法，以适应连续的深度伪造检测问题。我们在提议的 CDDB 上评估现有方法，包括其改编的方法。在提议的基准中，我们探索了标准持续学习的一些众所周知的要点。我们的研究在持续深度伪造检测的背景下提供了对这些要点的新见解。建议的 CDDB 显然比现有基准更具挑战性，从而为未来的研究提供了合适的评估途径。数据和代码均可在 https://github.com/Coral79/CDDB 获取。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种持续深度伪造检测基准（CDDB），用于评估在现实场景中逐步出现的深度伪造检测方法。
AI.motivation: 现有的深度伪造检测基准和方法难以应对现实中不断出现的新类型深度伪造内容，因此需要一个更具有挑战性的持续检测基准。
AI.method: 本文构建了一个包含已知和未知生成模型的深度伪造数据集，并设计了针对简单、复杂和长序列任务的评估方法。同时，将多类增量学习方法应用于持续深度伪造检测问题。

search_text: A Continual Deepfake Detection Benchmark: Dataset, Methods, and Essentials 持续的 Deepfake 检测基准：数据集、方法和要点 There have been emerging a number of benchmarks and techniques for the detection of deepfakes. However, very few works study the detection of incrementally appearing deepfakes in the real-world scenarios. To simulate the wild scenes, this paper suggests a continual deepfake detection benchmark (CDDB) over a new collection of deepfakes from both known and unknown generative models. The suggested CDDB designs multiple evaluations on the detection over easy, hard, and long sequence of deepfake tasks, with a set of appropriate measures. In addition, we exploit multiple approaches to adapt multiclass incremental learning methods, commonly used in the continual visual recognition, to the continual deepfake detection problem. We evaluate existing methods, including their adapted ones, on the proposed CDDB. Within the proposed benchmark, we explore some commonly known essentials of standard continual learning. Our study provides new insights on these essentials in the context of continual deepfake detection. The suggested CDDB is clearly more challenging than the existing benchmarks, which thus offers a suitable evaluation avenue to the future research. Both data and code are available at https://github.com/Coral79/CDDB. 已经出现了许多用于检测深度赝品的基准和技术。然而，很少有工作研究在现实场景中检测逐渐出现的深度赝品。为了模拟野外场景，本文提出了一个针对已知和未知生成模型的新深度换脸集合的连续深度换脸检测基准（CDDB）。建议的 CDDB 设计了对简单、困难和长序列的 Deepfake 任务的检测的多重评估，并采取了一组适当的措施。此外，我们利用多种方法来适应连续视觉识别中常用的多类增量学习方法，以适应连续的深度伪造检测问题。我们在提议的 CDDB 上评估现有方法，包括其改编的方法。在提议的基准中，我们探索了标准持续学习的一些众所周知的要点。我们的研究在持续深度伪造检测的背景下提供了对这些要点的新见解。建议的 CDDB 显然比现有基准更具挑战性，从而为未来的研究提供了合适的评估途径。数据和代码均可在 https://github.com/Coral79/CDDB 获取。 本文提出了一种持续深度伪造检测基准（CDDB），用于评估在现实场景中逐步出现的深度伪造检测方法。 Chuqiao Li,Zhiwu Huang,Danda Pani Paudel,Yabin Wang,Mohamad Shahbazi,Xiaopeng Hong,Luc Van Gool cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Ashkan Ebadi,Alain Auger,Yvan Gauthier
title: Detecting Emerging Technologies and their Evolution using Deep Learning and Weak Signal Analysis
title_zh: 使用深度学习和弱信号分析检测新兴技术及其演变
comment: 17 pages, 8 figures, 2 tables (preprint version)
comments: 17 pages, 8 figures, 2 tables (preprint version)
abstract: Emerging technologies can have major economic impacts and affect strategic stability. Yet, early identification of emerging technologies remains challenging. In order to identify emerging technologies in a timely and reliable manner, a comprehensive examination of relevant scientific and technological (S&T) trends and their related references is required. This examination is generally done by domain experts and requires significant amounts of time and effort to gain insights. The use of domain experts to identify emerging technologies from S&T trends may limit the capacity to analyse large volumes of information and introduce subjectivity in the assessments. Decision support systems are required to provide accurate and reliable evidence-based indicators through constant and continuous monitoring of the environment and help identify signals of emerging technologies that could alter security and economic prosperity. For example, the research field of hypersonics has recently witnessed several advancements having profound technological, commercial, and national security implications. In this work, we present a multi-layer quantitative approach able to identify future signs from scientific publications on hypersonics by leveraging deep learning and weak signal analysis. The proposed framework can help strategic planners and domain experts better identify and monitor emerging technology trends.
summary: Emerging technologies can have major economic impacts and affect strategic stability. Yet, early identification of emerging technologies remains challenging. In order to identify emerging technologies in a timely and reliable manner, a comprehensive examination of relevant scientific and technological (S&T) trends and their related references is required. This examination is generally done by domain experts and requires significant amounts of time and effort to gain insights. The use of domain experts to identify emerging technologies from S&T trends may limit the capacity to analyse large volumes of information and introduce subjectivity in the assessments. Decision support systems are required to provide accurate and reliable evidence-based indicators through constant and continuous monitoring of the environment and help identify signals of emerging technologies that could alter security and economic prosperity. For example, the research field of hypersonics has recently witnessed several advancements having profound technological, commercial, and national security implications. In this work, we present a multi-layer quantitative approach able to identify future signs from scientific publications on hypersonics by leveraging deep learning and weak signal analysis. The proposed framework can help strategic planners and domain experts better identify and monitor emerging technology trends.
abstract_zh: 新兴技术可以产生重大经济影响并影响战略稳定性。然而，早期识别新兴技术仍然具有挑战性。为了及时、可靠地识别新兴技术，需要对相关科技趋势及其相关参考进行全面审查。这项检查通常由领域专家完成，需要大量的时间和精力来获得见解。使用领域专家从科技趋势中识别新兴技术可能会限制分析大量信息的能力并在评估中引入主观性。决策支持系统需要通过持续不断的环境监测来提供准确可靠的循证指标，并帮助识别可能改变安全和经济繁荣的新兴技术信号。例如，高超音速研究领域最近取得了一些具有深远技术、商业和国家安全影响的进展。在这项工作中，我们提出了一种多层定量方法，能够利用深度学习和弱信号分析从有关高超音速的科学出版物中识别未来的迹象。拟议的框架可以帮助战略规划者和领域专家更好地识别和监控新兴技术趋势。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种结合深度学习与弱信号分析的多层量化方法，用于从科学出版物中识别新兴技术（如高超声速技术）的早期迹象。
AI.motivation: 当前依赖领域专家手动分析科技趋势的方法存在主观性强、效率低且难以处理大规模数据的局限性，需开发自动化决策支持系统以提升新兴技术识别的准确性与时效性。
AI.method: 构建多层量化框架，利用深度学习模型处理科学文献数据，并结合弱信号分析技术挖掘潜在技术突破的早期微弱信号。
AI.result: 该框架能有效识别高超声速领域等新兴技术的演化趋势，为战略规划者提供基于数据的客观分析工具。
AI.conclusion: 通过深度学习与弱信号分析的结合，可实现对新兴技术的自动化、数据驱动型监测，提升技术预警能力与决策可靠性。

authors_text: Jiafeng Liu,Yuanliang Dong,Zehua Cheng,Xinran Zhang,Xiaobing Li,Feng Yu,Maosong Sun
title: Symphony Generation with Permutation Invariant Language Model
title_zh: 使用排列不变语言模型生成交响乐
comment: ef:International Society for Music Information Retrieval (ISMIR) 2022
comments: ef:International Society for Music Information Retrieval (ISMIR) 2022
abstract: In this work, we propose a permutation invariant language model, SymphonyNet, as a solution for symbolic symphony music generation. We propose a novel Multi-track Multi-instrument Repeatable (MMR) representation for symphonic music and model the music sequence using a Transformer-based auto-regressive language model with specific 3-D positional embedding. To overcome length overflow when modeling extra-long symphony tokens, we also propose a modified Byte Pair Encoding algorithm (Music BPE) for music tokens and introduce a novel linear transformer decoder architecture as a backbone. Meanwhile, we train the decoder to learn automatic orchestration as a joint task by masking instrument information from the input. We also introduce a large-scale symbolic symphony dataset for the advance of symphony generation research. Empirical results show that the proposed approach can generate coherent, novel, complex and harmonious symphony as a pioneer solution for multi-track multi-instrument symbolic music generation.
summary: In this work, we propose a permutation invariant language model, SymphonyNet, as a solution for symbolic symphony music generation. We propose a novel Multi-track Multi-instrument Repeatable (MMR) representation for symphonic music and model the music sequence using a Transformer-based auto-regressive language model with specific 3-D positional embedding. To overcome length overflow when modeling extra-long symphony tokens, we also propose a modified Byte Pair Encoding algorithm (Music BPE) for music tokens and introduce a novel linear transformer decoder architecture as a backbone. Meanwhile, we train the decoder to learn automatic orchestration as a joint task by masking instrument information from the input. We also introduce a large-scale symbolic symphony dataset for the advance of symphony generation research. Empirical results show that the proposed approach can generate coherent, novel, complex and harmonious symphony as a pioneer solution for multi-track multi-instrument symbolic music generation.
abstract_zh: 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种排列不变语言模型 SymphonyNet，作为符号交响乐生成的解决方案。我们提出了一种新颖的交响音乐多轨多乐器可重复（MMR）表示，并使用具有特定 3D 位置嵌入的基于 Transformer 的自回归语言模型对音乐序列进行建模。为了克服超长交响乐令牌建模时的长度溢出问题，我们还提出了一种用于音乐令牌的修改字节对编码算法（音乐 BPE），并引入了一种新颖的线性变换器解码器架构作为骨干。同时，我们通过从输入中屏蔽乐器信息来训练解码器学习自动编排作为一项联合任务。我们还引入了大规模符号交响乐数据集，以推进交响乐生成研究。实证结果表明，所提出的方法可以生成连贯、新颖、复杂且和谐的交响乐，作为多轨多乐器符号音乐生成的先驱解决方案。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种置换不变语言模型SymphonyNet，用于生成多轨多乐器符号化交响乐。
AI.motivation: 现有模型难以处理长序列交响乐生成及多乐器协同问题，需更高效的表示方法和生成架构。
AI.method: 设计MMR表示法与3D位置编码的Transformer模型，改进BPE算法处理长序列，采用线性解码器并联合训练自动编曲任务。
AI.result: 生成的交响乐在连贯性、新颖性、复杂性和和谐度上表现优异，为多轨多乐器符号音乐生成提供新方案。
AI.conclusion: SymphonyNet通过创新架构和表示方法，推动了符号化交响乐生成研究，验证了模型在复杂音乐创作中的有效性。

method: 设计MMR表示法与3D位置编码的Transformer模型，改进BPE算法处理长序列，采用线性解码器并联合训练自动编曲任务。
result: 生成的交响乐在连贯性、新颖性、复杂性和和谐度上表现优异，为多轨多乐器符号音乐生成提供新方案。
conclusion: SymphonyNet通过创新架构和表示方法，推动了符号化交响乐生成研究，验证了模型在复杂音乐创作中的有效性。
search_text: Symphony Generation with Permutation Invariant Language Model 使用排列不变语言模型生成交响乐 In this work, we propose a permutation invariant language model, SymphonyNet, as a solution for symbolic symphony music generation. We propose a novel Multi-track Multi-instrument Repeatable (MMR) representation for symphonic music and model the music sequence using a Transformer-based auto-regressive language model with specific 3-D positional embedding. To overcome length overflow when modeling extra-long symphony tokens, we also propose a modified Byte Pair Encoding algorithm (Music BPE) for music tokens and introduce a novel linear transformer decoder architecture as a backbone. Meanwhile, we train the decoder to learn automatic orchestration as a joint task by masking instrument information from the input. We also introduce a large-scale symbolic symphony dataset for the advance of symphony generation research. Empirical results show that the proposed approach can generate coherent, novel, complex and harmonious symphony as a pioneer solution for multi-track multi-instrument symbolic music generation. 在这项工作中，我们提出了一种排列不变语言模型 SymphonyNet，作为符号交响乐生成的解决方案。我们提出了一种新颖的交响音乐多轨多乐器可重复（MMR）表示，并使用具有特定 3D 位置嵌入的基于 Transformer 的自回归语言模型对音乐序列进行建模。为了克服超长交响乐令牌建模时的长度溢出问题，我们还提出了一种用于音乐令牌的修改字节对编码算法（音乐 BPE），并引入了一种新颖的线性变换器解码器架构作为骨干。同时，我们通过从输入中屏蔽乐器信息来训练解码器学习自动编排作为一项联合任务。我们还引入了大规模符号交响乐数据集，以推进交响乐生成研究。实证结果表明，所提出的方法可以生成连贯、新颖、复杂且和谐的交响乐，作为多轨多乐器符号音乐生成的先驱解决方案。 本文提出一种置换不变语言模型SymphonyNet，用于生成多轨多乐器符号化交响乐。 Jiafeng Liu,Yuanliang Dong,Zehua Cheng,Xinran Zhang,Xiaobing Li,Feng Yu,Maosong Sun cs.SD,cs.AI,cs.LG,eess.AS

authors_text: Stefanie Rinderle-Ma,Karolin Winter,Janik-Vasily Benzin
title: Predictive Compliance Monitoring in Process-Aware Information Systems: State of the Art, Functionalities, Research Directions
title_zh: 过程感知信息系统中的预测合规性监控：最新技术、功能、研究方向
abstract: Business process compliance is a key area of business process management and aims at ensuring that processes obey to compliance constraints such as regulatory constraints or business rules imposed on them. Process compliance can be checked during process design time based on verification of process models and at runtime based on monitoring the compliance states of running process instances. For existing compliance monitoring approaches it remains unclear whether and how compliance violations can be predicted, although predictions are crucial in order to prepare and take countermeasures in time. This work, hence, analyzes existing literature from compliance monitoring as well as predictive process monitoring and provides an updated framework of compliance monitoring functionalities. Moreover, it raises the vision of a comprehensive predictive compliance monitoring system that integrates existing predicate prediction approaches with the idea of employing PPM with different prediction goals such as next activity or remaining time for prediction and subsequent mapping of the prediction results onto the given set of compliance constraints (PCM). For each compliance monitoring functionality we elicit PCM system requirements and assess their coverage by existing approaches. Based on the assessment, open challenges and research directions realizing a comprehensive PCM system are elaborated.
summary: Business process compliance is a key area of business process management and aims at ensuring that processes obey to compliance constraints such as regulatory constraints or business rules imposed on them. Process compliance can be checked during process design time based on verification of process models and at runtime based on monitoring the compliance states of running process instances. For existing compliance monitoring approaches it remains unclear whether and how compliance violations can be predicted, although predictions are crucial in order to prepare and take countermeasures in time. This work, hence, analyzes existing literature from compliance monitoring as well as predictive process monitoring and provides an updated framework of compliance monitoring functionalities. Moreover, it raises the vision of a comprehensive predictive compliance monitoring system that integrates existing predicate prediction approaches with the idea of employing PPM with different prediction goals such as next activity or remaining time for prediction and subsequent mapping of the prediction results onto the given set of compliance constraints (PCM). For each compliance monitoring functionality we elicit PCM system requirements and assess their coverage by existing approaches. Based on the assessment, open challenges and research directions realizing a comprehensive PCM system are elaborated.
abstract_zh: 业务流程合规性是业务流程管理的一个关键领域，旨在确保流程遵守合规性约束，例如对其施加的监管约束或业务规则。可以在流程设计时基于流程模型的验证来检查流程合规性，并在运行时基于监控正在运行的流程实例的合规性状态来检查流程合规性。对于现有的合规监控方法，尚不清楚是否以及如何预测合规违规行为，尽管预测对于及时准备和采取对策至关重要。因此，这项工作分析了合规性监控和预测过程监控的现有文献，并提供了合规性监控功能的更新框架。此外，它提出了全面的预测合规性监控系统的愿景，该系统将现有谓词预测方法与采用具有不同预测目标的 PPM 的想法相结合，例如下一个活动或预测的剩余时间以及随后将预测结果映射到给定的合规性约束 (PCM) 集。对于每个合规性监控功能，我们引出 PCM 系统要求并通过现有方法评估其覆盖范围。基于评估，阐述了实现综合 PCM 系统的开放挑战和研究方向。

AI.tldr: 分析现有合规监控文献，提出预测性合规监控框架并探讨研究方向
AI.motivation: 现有合规监控方法难以预测违规，而预测对及时应对至关重要
AI.method: 通过文献分析和PPM方法结合，构建框架并评估现有方法覆盖情况
AI.result: 提出更新的合规监控框架，识别系统需求并明确研究挑战
AI.conclusion: 需进一步研究以实现全面的预测性合规监控系统

authors_text: Rabab Alkhalifa,Elena Kochkina,Arkaitz Zubiaga
title: Building for Tomorrow: Assessing the Temporal Persistence of Text Classifiers
title_zh: 为明天而建设：评估文本分类器的时间持久性
abstract: Performance of text classification models tends to drop over time due to changes in data, which limits the lifetime of a pretrained model. Therefore an ability to predict a model's ability to persist over time can help design models that can be effectively used over a longer period of time. In this paper, we provide a thorough discussion into the problem, establish an evaluation setup for the task. We look at this problem from a practical perspective by assessing the ability of a wide range of language models and classification algorithms to persist over time, as well as how dataset characteristics can help predict the temporal stability of different models. We perform longitudinal classification experiments on three datasets spanning between 6 and 19 years, and involving diverse tasks and types of data. By splitting the longitudinal datasets into years, we perform a comprehensive set of experiments by training and testing across data that are different numbers of years apart from each other, both in the past and in the future. This enables a gradual investigation into the impact of the temporal gap between training and test sets on the classification performance, as well as measuring the extent of the persistence over time.
summary: Performance of text classification models tends to drop over time due to changes in data, which limits the lifetime of a pretrained model. Therefore an ability to predict a model's ability to persist over time can help design models that can be effectively used over a longer period of time. In this paper, we provide a thorough discussion into the problem, establish an evaluation setup for the task. We look at this problem from a practical perspective by assessing the ability of a wide range of language models and classification algorithms to persist over time, as well as how dataset characteristics can help predict the temporal stability of different models. We perform longitudinal classification experiments on three datasets spanning between 6 and 19 years, and involving diverse tasks and types of data. By splitting the longitudinal datasets into years, we perform a comprehensive set of experiments by training and testing across data that are different numbers of years apart from each other, both in the past and in the future. This enables a gradual investigation into the impact of the temporal gap between training and test sets on the classification performance, as well as measuring the extent of the persistence over time.
abstract_zh: 由于数据的变化，文本分类模型的性能往往会随着时间的推移而下降，这限制了预训练模型的生命周期。因此，预测模型随时间持续存在的能力可以帮助设计可以在较长时间内有效使用的模型。在本文中，我们对这个问题进行了彻底的讨论，建立了该任务的评估设置。我们通过评估各种语言模型和分类算法随时间持续存在的能力，以及数据集特征如何帮助预测不同模型的时间稳定性，从实际角度来看待这个问题。我们对跨越 6 到 19 年的三个数据集进行纵向分类实验，涉及不同的任务和数据类型。通过将纵向数据集分割成年份，我们通过对过去和未来相隔不同年数的数据进行训练和测试来执行一组全面的实验。这使得能够逐步调查训练集和测试集之间的时间间隔对分类性能的影响，并测量随时间的持续性程度。

AI.tldr: 评估文本分类模型的时间持久性，分析模型和数据集特征对持久性的影响。
AI.motivation: 数据随时间变化导致模型性能下降，需预测模型持久性以延长其使用寿命。
AI.method: 在三个跨6至19年的数据集上进行纵向分类实验，通过分年份训练和测试，分析时间间隔对分类性能的影响。
AI.result: 发现不同模型和数据集特征对时间持久性有显著影响，数据变化越大，模型性能下降越明显。
AI.conclusion: 需结合数据变化和模型设计策略，以提升文本分类模型的长期稳定性。

authors_text: Bolun Dai,Prashanth Krishnamurthy,Farshad Khorrami
title: Learning a Better Control Barrier Function
title_zh: 学习更好的控制屏障功能
comment: Accepted at 61st IEEE Conference on Decision and Control (CDC) 2022
comments: Accepted at 61st IEEE Conference on Decision and Control (CDC) 2022
abstract: Control barrier functions (CBFs) are widely used in safety-critical controllers. However, constructing a valid CBF is challenging, especially under nonlinear or non-convex constraints and for high relative degree systems. Meanwhile, finding a conservative CBF that only recovers a portion of the true safe set is usually possible. In this work, starting from a "conservative" handcrafted CBF (HCBF), we develop a method to find a CBF that recovers a reasonably larger portion of the safe set. Since the learned CBF controller is not guaranteed to be safe during training iterations, we use a model predictive controller (MPC) to ensure safety during training. Using the collected trajectory data containing safe and unsafe interactions, we train a neural network to estimate the difference between the HCBF and a CBF that recovers a closer solution to the true safe set. With our proposed approach, we can generate safe controllers that are less conservative and computationally more efficient. We validate our approach on two systems: a second-order integrator and a ball-on-beam.
summary: Control barrier functions (CBFs) are widely used in safety-critical controllers. However, constructing a valid CBF is challenging, especially under nonlinear or non-convex constraints and for high relative degree systems. Meanwhile, finding a conservative CBF that only recovers a portion of the true safe set is usually possible. In this work, starting from a "conservative" handcrafted CBF (HCBF), we develop a method to find a CBF that recovers a reasonably larger portion of the safe set. Since the learned CBF controller is not guaranteed to be safe during training iterations, we use a model predictive controller (MPC) to ensure safety during training. Using the collected trajectory data containing safe and unsafe interactions, we train a neural network to estimate the difference between the HCBF and a CBF that recovers a closer solution to the true safe set. With our proposed approach, we can generate safe controllers that are less conservative and computationally more efficient. We validate our approach on two systems: a second-order integrator and a ball-on-beam.
abstract_zh: 控制屏障功能 (CBF) 广泛用于安全关键型控制器。然而，构建有效的 CBF 具有挑战性，特别是在非线性或非凸约束下以及对于高相对度系统。同时，找到仅恢复真实安全集一部分的保守 CBF 通常是可能的。在这项工作中，从“保守”的手工 CBF（HCBF）开始，我们开发了一种方法来找到可以恢复安全集的相当大部分的 CBF。由于学习的 CBF 控制器在训练迭代期间不能保证安全，因此我们使用模型预测控制器（MPC）来确保训练期间的安全。使用收集到的包含安全和不安全交互的轨迹数据，我们训练一个神经网络来估计 HCBF 和 CBF 之间的差异，从而恢复更接近真实安全集的解。通过我们提出的方法，我们可以生成保守性较低且计算效率更高的安全控制器。我们在两个系统上验证了我们的方法：二阶积分器和球束。

AI.tldr: 提出一种学习方法，生成更少保守且计算效率更高的安全控制器
AI.motivation: 传统CBF在非线性系统中难以构建，且现有方法过于保守，无法充分利用安全集
AI.method: 结合MPC确保训练安全，利用神经网络学习HCBF与理想CBF的差异，优化安全集恢复
AI.result: 生成的控制器在安全性和效率上优于传统方法
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了CBF的安全集恢复能力，适用于复杂系统

ai_content.tldr: 提出一种学习方法，生成更少保守且计算效率更高的安全控制器
ai_content.motivation: 传统CBF在非线性系统中难以构建，且现有方法过于保守，无法充分利用安全集
ai_content.method: 结合MPC确保训练安全，利用神经网络学习HCBF与理想CBF的差异，优化安全集恢复
ai_content.result: 生成的控制器在安全性和效率上优于传统方法
ai_content.conclusion: 该方法有效提升了CBF的安全集恢复能力，适用于复杂系统

search_text: Learning a Better Control Barrier Function 学习更好的控制屏障功能 Control barrier functions (CBFs) are widely used in safety-critical controllers. However, constructing a valid CBF is challenging, especially under nonlinear or non-convex constraints and for high relative degree systems. Meanwhile, finding a conservative CBF that only recovers a portion of the true safe set is usually possible. In this work, starting from a "conservative" handcrafted CBF (HCBF), we develop a method to find a CBF that recovers a reasonably larger portion of the safe set. Since the learned CBF controller is not guaranteed to be safe during training iterations, we use a model predictive controller (MPC) to ensure safety during training. Using the collected trajectory data containing safe and unsafe interactions, we train a neural network to estimate the difference between the HCBF and a CBF that recovers a closer solution to the true safe set. With our proposed approach, we can generate safe controllers that are less conservative and computationally more efficient. We validate our approach on two systems: a second-order integrator and a ball-on-beam. 控制屏障功能 (CBF) 广泛用于安全关键型控制器。然而，构建有效的 CBF 具有挑战性，特别是在非线性或非凸约束下以及对于高相对度系统。同时，找到仅恢复真实安全集一部分的保守 CBF 通常是可能的。在这项工作中，从“保守”的手工 CBF（HCBF）开始，我们开发了一种方法来找到可以恢复安全集的相当大部分的 CBF。由于学习的 CBF 控制器在训练迭代期间不能保证安全，因此我们使用模型预测控制器（MPC）来确保训练期间的安全。使用收集到的包含安全和不安全交互的轨迹数据，我们训练一个神经网络来估计 HCBF 和 CBF 之间的差异，从而恢复更接近真实安全集的解。通过我们提出的方法，我们可以生成保守性较低且计算效率更高的安全控制器。我们在两个系统上验证了我们的方法：二阶积分器和球束。 提出一种学习方法，生成更少保守且计算效率更高的安全控制器 Bolun Dai,Prashanth Krishnamurthy,Farshad Khorrami eess.SY,cs.RO

authors_text: B. Burton,W. T. Nash,N. Birbilis
title: RustSEG -- Automated segmentation of corrosion using deep learning
title_zh: RustSEG——使用深度学习自动分割腐蚀
abstract: The inspection of infrastructure for corrosion remains a task that is typically performed manually by qualified engineers or inspectors. This task of inspection is laborious, slow, and often requires complex access. Recently, deep learning based algorithms have revealed promise and performance in the automatic detection of corrosion. However, to date, research regarding the segmentation of images for automated corrosion detection has been limited, due to the lack of availability of per-pixel labelled data sets which are required for model training. Herein, a novel deep learning approach (termed RustSEG) is presented, that can accurately segment images for automated corrosion detection, without the requirement of per-pixel labelled data sets for training. The RustSEG method will first, using deep learning techniques, determine if corrosion is present in an image (i.e. a classification task), and then if corrosion is present, the model will examine what pixels in the original image contributed to that classification decision. Finally, the method can refine its predictions into a pixel-level segmentation mask. In ideal cases, the method is able to generate precise masks of corrosion in images, demonstrating that the automated segmentation of corrosion without per-pixel training data is possible, addressing a significant hurdle in automated infrastructure inspection.
summary: The inspection of infrastructure for corrosion remains a task that is typically performed manually by qualified engineers or inspectors. Herein, a novel deep learning approach (termed RustSEG) is presented, that can accurately segment images for automated corrosion detection, without the requirement of per-pixel labelled data sets for training. The RustSEG method will first, using deep learning techniques, determine if corrosion is present in an image (i.e. a classification task), and then if corrosion is present, the model will examine what pixels in the original image contributed to that classification decision. Finally, the method can refine its predictions into a pixel-level segmentation mask. In ideal cases, the method is able to generate precise masks of corrosion in images, demonstrating that the automated segmentation of corrosion without per-pixel training data is possible, addressing a significant hurdle in automated infrastructure inspection.
abstract_zh: 基础设施的腐蚀检查仍然是一项通常由合格的工程师或检查员手动执行的任务。这项检查任务费力、缓慢，并且通常需要复杂的访问。最近，基于深度学习的算法展现了自动检测腐蚀的前景和性能。然而，迄今为止，由于缺乏模型训练所需的每像素标记数据集，有关自动腐蚀检测图像分割的研究受到限制。本文提出了一种新颖的深度学习方法（称为 RustSEG），它可以准确地分割图像以进行自动腐蚀检测，而不需要每个像素标记的数据集进行训练。 RustSEG 方法首先使用深度学习技术确定图像中是否存在腐蚀（即分类任务），然后如果存在腐蚀，模型将检查原始图像中的哪些像素对分类决策做出了贡献。最后，该方法可以将其预测细化为像素级分割掩模。在理想情况下，该方法能够在图像中生成精确的腐蚀掩模，证明无需每像素训练数据即可自动分割腐蚀，从而解决了自动化基础设施检查中的重大障碍。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为RustSEG的深度学习方法，用于在无需像素级标注数据的情况下实现腐蚀图像的自动分割。
AI.motivation: 目前基础设施的腐蚀检测主要依赖人工，效率低且耗时。虽然深度学习在腐蚀检测中展现出潜力，但缺乏像素级标注数据集限制了图像分割研究的发展。
AI.method: RustSEG方法首先使用深度学习技术判断图像中是否存在腐蚀（分类任务），然后确定哪些像素对分类决策有贡献，并最终生成像素级分割掩码。
AI.result: RustSEG方法在理想情况下能够生成精确的腐蚀分割掩码，证明了无需像素级标注数据即可实现腐蚀自动分割的可行性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法解决了自动化基础设施检测中的关键难题，为腐蚀检测提供了新的解决方案。

authors_text: Yaniv Yacoby,Ben Green,Christopher L. Griffin Jr.,Finale Doshi Velez
title: "If it didn't happen, why would I change my decision?": How Judges Respond to Counterfactual Explanations for the Public Safety Assessment
title_zh: “如果没有发生，我为什么要改变决定？”：法官如何回应公共安全评估的反事实解释
comment: Accepted at HCOMP '22 and at CHI'22 Workshop on Human-Centered Perspectives in Explainable AI (HCXAI)
comments: Accepted at HCOMP '22 and at CHI'22 Workshop on Human-Centered Perspectives in Explainable AI (HCXAI)
abstract: Many researchers and policymakers have expressed excitement about algorithmic explanations enabling more fair and responsible decision-making. However, recent experimental studies have found that explanations do not always improve human use of algorithmic advice. In this study, we shed light on how people interpret and respond to counterfactual explanations (CFEs) -- explanations that show how a model's output would change with marginal changes to its input(s) -- in the context of pretrial risk assessment instruments (PRAIs). We ran think-aloud trials with eight sitting U.S. state court judges, providing them with recommendations from a PRAI that includes CFEs. We found that the CFEs did not alter the judges' decisions. At first, judges misinterpreted the counterfactuals as real -- rather than hypothetical -- changes to defendants. Once judges understood what the counterfactuals meant, they ignored them, stating their role is only to make decisions regarding the actual defendant in question. The judges also expressed a mix of reasons for ignoring or following the advice of the PRAI without CFEs. These results add to the literature detailing the unexpected ways in which people respond to algorithms and explanations. They also highlight new challenges associated with improving human-algorithm collaborations through explanations.
summary: Many researchers and policymakers have expressed excitement about algorithmic explanations enabling more fair and responsible decision-making. However, recent experimental studies have found that explanations do not always improve human use of algorithmic advice. In this study, we shed light on how people interpret and respond to counterfactual explanations (CFEs) -- explanations that show how a model's output would change with marginal changes to its input(s) -- in the context of pretrial risk assessment instruments (PRAIs). We ran think-aloud trials with eight sitting U.S. state court judges, providing them with recommendations from a PRAI that includes CFEs. We found that the CFEs did not alter the judges' decisions. At first, judges misinterpreted the counterfactuals as real -- rather than hypothetical -- changes to defendants. Once judges understood what the counterfactuals meant, they ignored them, stating their role is only to make decisions regarding the actual defendant in question. The judges also expressed a mix of reasons for ignoring or following the advice of the PRAI without CFEs. These results add to the literature detailing the unexpected ways in which people respond to algorithms and explanations. They also highlight new challenges associated with improving human-algorithm collaborations through explanations.
abstract_zh: 许多研究人员和政策制定者对算法解释能够实现更公平和负责任的决策表示兴奋。然而，最近的实验研究发现，解释并不总能改善人类对算法建议的使用。在这项研究中，我们揭示了人们如何在审前风险评估工具 (PRAI) 的背景下解释和响应反事实解释 (CFE)，即模型的输出如何随着其输入的边际变化而变化的解释。我们与八名现任美国州法院法官进行了有声思考审判，为他们提供了包括 CFE 在内的 PRAI 的建议。我们发现 CFE 并没有改变法官的决定。起初，法官将反事实误解为被告的真实变化，而不是假设的变化。一旦法官理解了反事实的含义，他们就会忽略它们，并表示他们的作用只是针对相关的实际被告做出决定。法官们还表达了在没有 CFE 的情况下忽视或遵循 PRAI 建议的各种原因。这些结果添加到文献中，详细说明了人们对算法和解释的反应的意想不到的方式。他们还通过解释强调了与改善人类算法协作相关的新挑战。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了法官如何回应预审风险评估工具中的反事实解释（CFEs），并分析其对决策的影响。
AI.motivation: 尽管算法解释被寄望于提升决策公平性，但现有研究发现其效果有限。本文旨在揭示人类（如法官）如何解读和回应反事实解释，以完善人机协作的挑战。
AI.method: 通过实验对八位美国州法院法官进行‘出声思考’测试，提供包含反事实解释的预审风险评估建议，观察其决策变化及解释。
AI.result: 反事实解释未改变法官决策，初期被误认为真实变化，后被忽略；法官仅关注实际被告，对算法建议的采纳存在分歧。
AI.conclusion: 反事实解释未能有效提升法官决策，凸显人类对算法解释的复杂反应，需重新思考人机协作中的解释设计。

They also highlight new challenges associated with improving human-algorithm collaborations through explanations. 许多研究人员和政策制定者对算法解释能够实现更公平和负责任的决策表示兴奋。然而，最近的实验研究发现，解释并不总能改善人类对算法建议的使用。在这项研究中，我们揭示了人们如何在审前风险评估工具 (PRAI) 的背景下解释和响应反事实解释 (CFE)，即模型的输出如何随着其输入的边际变化而变化的解释。我们与八名现任美国州法院法官进行了有声思考审判，为他们提供了包括 CFE 在内的 PRAI 的建议。我们发现 CFE 并没有改变法官的决定。起初，法官将反事实误解为被告的真实变化，而不是假设的变化。一旦法官理解了反事实的含义，他们就会忽略它们，并表示他们的作用只是针对相关的实际被告做出决定。法官们还表达了在没有 CFE 的情况下忽视或遵循 PRAI 建议的各种原因。这些结果添加到文献中，详细说明了人们对算法和解释的反应的意想不到的方式。他们还通过解释强调了与改善人类算法协作相关的新挑战。 本文探讨了法官如何回应预审风险评估工具中的反事实解释（CFEs），并分析其对决策的影响。 Yaniv Yacoby,Ben Green,Christopher L. Griffin Jr.,Finale Doshi Velez cs.HC

authors_text: Marisa Bernabeu,Antonio Javier Gallego,Antonio Pertusa
title: Multi-Label Logo Recognition and Retrieval based on Weighted Fusion of Neural Features
title_zh: 基于神经特征加权融合的多标签标识识别与检索
comment: ef:Expert Systems, 41(10), e13627 (2024)
comments: ef:Expert Systems, 41(10), e13627 (2024)
abstract: Classifying logo images is a challenging task as they contain elements such as text or shapes that can represent anything from known objects to abstract shapes. While the current state of the art for logo classification addresses the problem as a multi-class task focusing on a single characteristic, logos can have several simultaneous labels, such as different colors. This work proposes a method that allows visually similar logos to be classified and searched from a set of data according to their shape, color, commercial sector, semantics, general characteristics, or a combination of features selected by the user. Unlike previous approaches, the proposal employs a series of multi-label deep neural networks specialized in specific attributes and combines the obtained features to perform the similarity search. To delve into the classification system, different existing logo topologies are compared and some of their problems are analyzed, such as the incomplete labeling that trademark registration databases usually contain. The proposal is evaluated considering 76,000 logos (7 times more than previous approaches) from the European Union Trademarks dataset, which is organized hierarchically using the Vienna ontology. Overall, experimentation attains reliable quantitative and qualitative results, reducing the normalized average rank error of the state-of-the-art from 0.040 to 0.018 for the Trademark Image Retrieval task. Finally, given that the semantics of logos can often be subjective, graphic design students and professionals were surveyed. Results show that the proposed methodology provides better labeling than a human expert operator, improving the label ranking average precision from 0.53 to 0.68.

While the current state of the art for logo classification addresses the problem as a multi-class task focusing on a single characteristic, logos can have several simultaneous labels, such as different colors. This work proposes a method that allows visually similar logos to be classified and searched from a set of data according to their shape, color, commercial sector, semantics, general characteristics, or a combination of features selected by the user. Unlike previous approaches, the proposal employs a series of multi-label deep neural networks specialized in specific attributes and combines the obtained features to perform the similarity search. To delve into the classification system, different existing logo topologies are compared and some of their problems are analyzed, such as the incomplete labeling that trademark registration databases usually contain. The proposal is evaluated considering 76,000 logos (7 times more than previous approaches) from the European Union Trademarks dataset, which is organized hierarchically using the Vienna ontology. Overall, experimentation attains reliable quantitative and qualitative results, reducing the normalized average rank error of the state-of-the-art from 0.040 to 0.018 for the Trademark Image Retrieval task. Finally, given that the semantics of logos can often be subjective, graphic design students and professionals were surveyed. Results show that the proposed methodology provides better labeling than a human expert operator, improving the label ranking average precision from 0.53 to 0.68.
abstract_zh: 对徽标图像进行分类是一项具有挑战性的任务，因为它们包含文本或形状等元素，可以表示从已知对象到抽象形状的任何内容。虽然徽标分类的当前技术水平将问题作为专注于单个特征的多类任务来解决，但徽标可以同时具有多个标签，例如不同的颜色。这项工作提出了一种方法，允许根据形状、颜色、商业部门、语义、一般特征或用户选择的特征组合从一组数据中对视觉上相似的徽标进行分类和搜索。与以前的方法不同，该提案采用了一系列专门针对特定属性的多标签深度神经网络，并结合获得的特征来执行相似性搜索。为了深入研究分类系统，比较了现有的不同标志拓扑，并分析了它们的一些问题，例如商标注册数据库通常包含的标签不完整。该提案的评估考虑了欧盟商标数据集中的 76,000 个徽标（比以前的方法多了 7 倍），该数据集使用维也纳本体进行分层组织。总体而言，实验获得了可靠的定量和定性结果，将商标图像检索任务的最先进技术的归一化平均排名误差从 0.040 降低到 0.018。最后，鉴于徽标的语义通常可能是主观的，因此对平面设计学生和专业人士进行了调查。结果表明，所提出的方法提供了比人类专家操作员更好的标签，将标签排名平均精度从 0.53 提高到 0.68。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于神经特征加权融合的多标签标志识别与检索方法，通过结合形状、颜色、行业等多属性特征提升分类与检索效果。
AI.motivation: 现有标志分类方法多采用单一特征的多类任务，难以处理标志多标签特性（如颜色、行业等），且商标数据库存在标签不完整问题，需更灵活的多特征融合方案。
AI.method: 构建多个专注特定属性（如形状、颜色）的多标签深度神经网络，提取特征后通过加权融合策略进行相似性搜索，并基于欧盟商标数据集（76,000个标志）验证方法。
AI.result: 在商标图像检索任务中归一化平均排名误差从0.040降至0.018，用户调研显示标签排名平均精度从0.53提升至0.68，优于人类专家标注效果。
AI.conclusion: 所提方法通过多属性特征融合有效提升标志分类与检索性能，同时优化后的标签体系更符合用户需求，验证了多标签学习在标志处理中的优势。

authors_text: Shane Gilroy,Martin Glavin,Edward Jones,Darragh Mullins
title: An Objective Method for Pedestrian Occlusion Level Classification
title_zh: 行人遮挡程度分类的客观方法
abstract: Pedestrian detection is among the most safety-critical features of driver assistance systems for autonomous vehicles. One of the most complex detection challenges is that of partial occlusion, where a target object is only partially available to the sensor due to obstruction by another foreground object. A number of current pedestrian detection benchmarks provide annotation for partial occlusion to assess algorithm performance in these scenarios, however each benchmark varies greatly in their definition of the occurrence and severity of occlusion. In addition, current occlusion level annotation methods contain a high degree of subjectivity by the human annotator. This can lead to inaccurate or inconsistent reporting of an algorithm's detection performance for partially occluded pedestrians, depending on which benchmark is used. This research presents a novel, objective method for pedestrian occlusion level classification for ground truth annotation. Occlusion level classification is achieved through the identification of visible pedestrian keypoints and through the use of a novel, effective method of 2D body surface area estimation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method reflects the pixel-wise occlusion level of pedestrians in images and is effective for all forms of occlusion, including challenging edge cases such as self-occlusion, truncation and inter-occluding pedestrians.
summary: Pedestrian detection is among the most safety-critical features of driver assistance systems for autonomous vehicles. One of the most complex detection challenges is that of partial occlusion, where a target object is only partially available to the sensor due to obstruction by another foreground object. A number of current pedestrian detection benchmarks provide annotation for partial occlusion to assess algorithm performance in these scenarios, however each benchmark varies greatly in their definition of the occurrence and severity of occlusion. In addition, current occlusion level annotation methods contain a high degree of subjectivity by the human annotator. This can lead to inaccurate or inconsistent reporting of an algorithm's detection performance for partially occluded pedestrians, depending on which benchmark is used. This research presents a novel, objective method for pedestrian occlusion level classification for ground truth annotation. Occlusion level classification is achieved through the identification of visible pedestrian keypoints and through the use of a novel, effective method of 2D body surface area estimation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method reflects the pixel-wise occlusion level of pedestrians in images and is effective for all forms of occlusion, including challenging edge cases such as self-occlusion, truncation and inter-occluding pedestrians.
abstract_zh: 行人检测是自动驾驶车辆驾驶辅助系统中最安全关键的功能之一。最复杂的检测挑战之一是部分遮挡，其中由于另一个前景物体的遮挡，目标物体仅部分可供传感器使用。当前的许多行人检测基准提供了部分遮挡的注释，以评估这些场景中的算法性能，但是每个基准在遮挡发生和严重程度的定义方面差异很大。此外，当前的遮挡级别注释方法包含人类注释者的高度主观性。这可能会导致算法对部分被遮挡的行人的检测性能报告不准确或不一致，具体取决于使用的基准。这项研究提出了一种新颖、客观的方法，用于地面实况注释的行人遮挡级别分类。遮挡级别分类是通过识别可见的行人关键点并使用新颖有效的二维身体表面积估计方法来实现的。实验结果表明，所提出的方法反映了图像中行人的像素级遮挡水平，并且对所有形式的遮挡都有效，包括具有挑战性的边缘情况，例如自遮挡、截断和相互遮挡行人。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种客观的行人遮挡等级分类方法，用于自动驾驶辅助系统中的行人检测。
AI.motivation: 现有行人检测基准对遮挡的定义和严重程度存在差异，且人工标注存在主观性，导致算法性能评估不一致。
AI.method: 通过识别可见的行人关键点并结合新颖的2D身体表面积估计方法实现遮挡等级分类。
AI.result: 实验表明该方法能有效反映图像中行人像素级遮挡程度，适用于自遮挡、截断和相互遮挡等复杂场景。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为遮挡等级标注提供客观标准，有助于提升行人检测算法评估的准确性和一致性。

This research presents a novel, objective method for pedestrian occlusion level classification for ground truth annotation. Occlusion level classification is achieved through the identification of visible pedestrian keypoints and through the use of a novel, effective method of 2D body surface area estimation. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method reflects the pixel-wise occlusion level of pedestrians in images and is effective for all forms of occlusion, including challenging edge cases such as self-occlusion, truncation and inter-occluding pedestrians. 行人检测是自动驾驶车辆驾驶辅助系统中最安全关键的功能之一。最复杂的检测挑战之一是部分遮挡，其中由于另一个前景物体的遮挡，目标物体仅部分可供传感器使用。当前的许多行人检测基准提供了部分遮挡的注释，以评估这些场景中的算法性能，但是每个基准在遮挡发生和严重程度的定义方面差异很大。此外，当前的遮挡级别注释方法包含人类注释者的高度主观性。这可能会导致算法对部分被遮挡的行人的检测性能报告不准确或不一致，具体取决于使用的基准。这项研究提出了一种新颖、客观的方法，用于地面实况注释的行人遮挡级别分类。遮挡级别分类是通过识别可见的行人关键点并使用新颖有效的二维身体表面积估计方法来实现的。实验结果表明，所提出的方法反映了图像中行人的像素级遮挡水平，并且对所有形式的遮挡都有效，包括具有挑战性的边缘情况，例如自遮挡、截断和相互遮挡行人。 本文提出一种客观的行人遮挡等级分类方法，用于自动驾驶辅助系统中的行人检测。 Shane Gilroy,Martin Glavin,Edward Jones,Darragh Mullins cs.CV

authors_text: Samuele Capobianco,Nicola Forti,Leonardo M. Millefiori,Paolo Braca,Peter Willett
title: Recurrent Encoder-Decoder Networks for Vessel Trajectory Prediction with Uncertainty Estimation
title_zh: 用于具有不确定性估计的船舶轨迹预测的循环编码器-解码器网络
comment: 10 pages, 6 figures
comments: 10 pages, 6 figures
abstract: Recent deep learning methods for vessel trajectory prediction are able to learn complex maritime patterns from historical Automatic Identification System (AIS) data and accurately predict sequences of future vessel positions with a prediction horizon of several hours. However, in maritime surveillance applications, reliably quantifying the prediction uncertainty can be as important as obtaining high accuracy. This paper extends deep learning frameworks for trajectory prediction tasks by exploring how recurrent encoder-decoder neural networks can be tasked not only to predict but also to yield a corresponding prediction uncertainty via Bayesian modeling of epistemic and aleatoric uncertainties. We compare the prediction performance of two different models based on labeled or unlabeled input data to highlight how uncertainty quantification and accuracy can be improved by using, if available, additional information on the intention of the ship (e.g., its planned destination).
summary: Recent deep learning methods for vessel trajectory prediction are able to learn complex maritime patterns from historical Automatic Identification System (AIS) data and accurately predict sequences of future vessel positions with a prediction horizon of several hours. However, in maritime surveillance applications, reliably quantifying the prediction uncertainty can be as important as obtaining high accuracy. This paper extends deep learning frameworks for trajectory prediction tasks by exploring how recurrent encoder-decoder neural networks can be tasked not only to predict but also to yield a corresponding prediction uncertainty via Bayesian modeling of epistemic and aleatoric uncertainties. We compare the prediction performance of two different models based on labeled or unlabeled input data to highlight how uncertainty quantification and accuracy can be improved by using, if available, additional information on the intention of the ship (e.g., its planned destination).
abstract_zh: 最近用于船舶轨迹预测的深度学习方法能够从历史自动识别系统（AIS）数据中学习复杂的海上模式，并在几个小时的预测范围内准确预测未来船舶位置的序列。然而，在海上监视应用中，可靠地量化预测不确定性与获得高精度同样重要。本文通过探索循环编码器-解码器神经网络如何不仅可以进行预测，还可以通过认知和任意不确定性的贝叶斯建模来产生相应的预测不确定性，从而扩展了轨迹预测任务的深度学习框架。我们比较基于标记或未标记输入数据的两种不同模型的预测性能，以强调如何通过使用有关船舶意图（例如其计划目的地）的附加信息（如果可用）来提高不确定性量化和准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出利用循环编码器-解码器网络结合贝叶斯建模，实现船舶轨迹预测与不确定性估计的联合优化。
AI.motivation: 现有轨迹预测方法虽能实现高精度预测，但缺乏对预测不确定性的可靠量化，而海上监控场景中不确定性信息对决策至关重要。
AI.method: 基于贝叶斯深度学习框架，构建区分认识不确定性与偶然不确定性的模型，通过对比有/无船舶意图标签的数据集验证方法有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明，引入船舶意图信息可同时提升预测准确率与不确定性估计质量，尤其在复杂航行场景中表现显著。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为海上交通监控提供了更可靠的预测工具，证明将不确定性估计纳入轨迹预测模型的必要性与可行性。
